game_description: Roll dice for points — but farkle (score nothing) and you lose your turn.

strategies.strategy_detailed: You must score 500+ in a single turn to get on the board. Don't bank with 350 hoping to add next turn — it resets. Push through low scores (200-400) to reach 500. Once on the board, conservative play becomes viable.
strategy_stealth: Must reach 500 in one turn to enter

strategies.strategy_detailed: With 3+ dice remaining, rolling again is statistically favorable — farkle probability is low enough that expected value favors rolling. With 2 dice, farkle risk jumps to ~44%. With 1 die, only a 1 or 5 scores; farkle risk is 67%. One die = almost always bank.

Hot dice let you reroll all 6 with your accumulated score safe. Always reroll on hot dice — the math strongly favors it. A hot dice roll from 600 points to 600+ adds expected value of 300-400 per reroll. Passing on hot dice is the most common Farkle mistake.
strategy_stealth: Passing hot dice = most common mistake

strategies.strategy_detailed: Target 750-1000 per turn in early rounds with no pressure. Below 750, you're giving up expected value by banking too soon. Above 1000, each additional roll risks a meaningful accumulated score. Calibrate your stop point to the round's risk context.

When an opponent is above 8000, play aggressively — bank nothing under 1000. You need big turns to catch up. Accept farkle risk; a safe 600-point turn when you need 2000 to catch up is effectively a wasted turn. Swing big or fall behind permanently.
strategy_stealth: Accept farkle risk to swing big

strategies.strategy_detailed: Triplets on the first roll are high-value sets (1s = 1000, 2s = 200, 3s = 300, etc.). Bank all 6 dice as a triplet pair when possible — that's 1000+ points. After banking a triplet, reroll remaining dice only if 3+ dice remain. Triplets are your primary scoring engine.
strategy_stealth: Triplet pair on first roll = 1000+ pts
Reroll remaining only if 3+ dice left

strategies.strategy_detailed: With a lead of 2000+, shift to conservative play. Bank at 750 and protect your position. The goal isn't maximum points — it's not farkling while maintaining the lead. A farkle from 9500 to 9500 (no progress) while opponents gain 1000 each is a momentum shift.
strategy_stealth: Farkle with big lead = momentum lost

strategies.strategy_detailed: Once a player hits 10000, everyone gets one final turn. You must beat their score to win. If 500 behind, play conservatively — likely still reachable. If 1500+ behind, roll aggressively until you either pass them or farkle out. There's no second final turn.
strategy_stealth: 1500+ behind: roll until pass or farkle

strategies.strategy_detailed: Maximize your final score before banking. Opponents get one turn each after you. A score of 10400 is much safer than 10050 — every extra 100 points increases the chance your score holds. If hot dice fire, keep rolling until farkle risk on 2 dice or you hit a comfortable lead.

1000-point deficit in the final round requires exactly one high-scoring turn. Start with aggressive rerolls until you pass their score. Once you've cleared their total, bank immediately — no need to pad further. One more roll risks losing a lead you already earned.
strategy_stealth: Reroll aggressively until you pass them
Bank immediately once you clear their score
One more roll after leads = unnecessary risk
